_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize Dallas TX local batch (lorie-blair, maxie-johnson, paul-e-ridley, paula-blackmon, zarin-gracey). LOCAL tier, social-media + local-press + council-minutes protocol. Currency verified for all five against the May/June 2025 Dallas council cycle (Ballotpedia + KERA/Dallas County canvass).

profile.background: 35 years at AT&T (customer service rep to IT project manager); Dallas City Plan Commissioner for D8 2020-2024 (Kleberg-Rylie land use, Floral Farms Area Plan, ForwardDallas 2.0); certified mediator. Won the June 7, 2025 D8 runoff 56.1%-43.9% over Erik Wilson, succeeding term-limited Tennell Atkins; seated June 16, 2025. D8 = southern/southeast Dallas (Kleberg-Rylie, Inland Port, Highland Hills, RedBird) — prior note wrongly said Lake Highlands.

lorie-blair.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: won June 7, 2025 runoff (56.14%, Dallas County canvass) over Erik Wilson; term ends 2027. Corrected stale background (she was a Plan Commissioner, not a DART board member; D8 is southern Dallas, not Lake Highlands). Thin paper trail: ABSENT for the Sept 17-18, 2025 FY2025-26 budget vote (left early, personal matter — no individual tax-rate vote recorded). Campaign site states she 'strongly supports law enforcement'; endorsed by police & fire organizations and Dallas County Sheriff Marian Brown (also SEIU Texas). Attended the Nov 6, 2025 joint-committee session that unanimously postponed/killed the $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership (13 of 14 members present, only West absent — she joined the unanimous rejection but issued no individual statement and was not among the October opposition signers). No findable positions on abortion, marriage/trans, schools, religious liberty, elections, state-overreach cells, or guns — left null. 2025 Stonewall Democrats D8 endorsement decision unresolved — not scored. Religion not stated in any source found.

0.note: Campaign platform affirmatively states she 'strongly supports law enforcement'; endorsed by police & fire organizations and Dallas County Sheriff Marian Brown. No defund-era record (seated June 2025). Affirmative self-stated backing, not merely a budget vote.

1.note: No individual statement on deportation policy found — left null.

2.note: Nov 6, 2025 joint-committee vote to indefinitely postpone the $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership was unanimous among the 13 of 14 councilmembers attending; only West was absent — Blair attended and joined the unanimous rejection of local-federal immigration-enforcement cooperation.

profile.religion: Baptist (senior pastor, New Morning Star Missionary Baptist Church, West Dallas)
profile.education: A. Maceo Smith HS; attended Southwestern Assemblies of God University
profile.background: Senior pastor of New Morning Star Missionary Baptist Church (West Dallas); Dallas ISD trustee District 5 2019-2025; lost his oldest son to gun violence in 2019; lost a 2021 bid for this D4 seat. Won D4 outright May 3, 2025 (~75%, no runoff), unseating incumbent Carolyn King Arnold; seated June 16, 2025. D4 = south Dallas/south Oak Cliff — prior note wrongly said Pleasant Grove.

maxie-johnson.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: won May 3, 2025 outright (~75%) over Alexander and Hardaman, unseating Carolyn King Arnold; seated June 16, 2025; chaired the joint Public Safety/Government Efficiency committee through the 287(g) fight. Corrected stale background (he is D4 south Oak Cliff, not Pleasant Grove; no runoff). Led the Nov 6, 2025 unanimous committee kill of the $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership with hard quotes: 'We're not gonna support racism, we're not gonna take money cause all money ain't good money' and 'it's hard for me to support any organization that rips apart homes based on skin color.' As DISD trustee, used his work email (Aug 2024) to declare 'I have always believed, and continue to advocate, that public funds belong in public schools,' opposing the Governor's school-choice push — documented contrary on school choice. Delivers openly Christian invocation prayer at council (Jan 14, 2026 session video). DISD board rejected SB 763 school chaplains 7-0 with one unnamed absence — his individual vote unconfirmed, not scored. Dallas Morning News + Dallas Neighbors for Housing endorsements 2025; Stonewall Democrats screening outcome unconfirmed — not scored. No findable positions on abortion, marriage/trans, elections, state-overreach, or guns — left null. FY2025-26 budget: not individually named in coverage — not scored.

0.note: As DISD trustee (Aug 2024): 'I have always believed, and continue to advocate, that public funds belong in public schools,' citing 'efforts by the Governor to defund our public schools' (the voucher/school-choice push) — documented contrary to universal school choice.
Serving Baptist pastor who prays publicly in office; no explicit statement on the RIGHT to profess Christ in all spheres found — left null.

3.note: Personally delivered the openly Christian invocation prayer at the Jan 14, 2026 council session ('Father in heaven, we come saying thank you...') — affirmative practice of prayer in public bodies.

0.note: Said public safety is a top D4 concern (Dec 2025 committee briefing) — constituent-concern relay, not affirmative pro-police advocacy; no defund-era record (seated June 2025). Null per the high-bar rule.

1.note: 'It's hard for me to support any organization that rips apart homes based on skin color' (re ICE, Nov 6, 2025) — documented contrary to supporting mandatory deportation.

2.note: Chaired the joint committee that voted unanimously Nov 6, 2025 to kill the $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership: 'we're not gonna take money cause all money ain't good money, and we're gonna support our chief of police' — documented contrary to local-federal immigration-enforcement cooperation.

profile.education: B.S. + M.Arch/urban planning, University of Michigan; J.D., University of Texas School of Law
profile.background: Retired construction-law/commercial-litigation attorney (30+ years); licensed architect; 8 years Dallas Plan Commission, 4 years Landmark Commission; Greater Dallas Planning Council president 2018-19. First elected June 2021; re-elected 2023; re-elected May 3, 2025 unopposed (100%) — third term, ends 2027. D14 = downtown/Uptown/East Dallas.

paul-e-ridley.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: re-elected May 3, 2025 unopposed (100%, the only unopposed council race). Seconded and voted YES on the Aug 10, 2022 GRACE Act (13-1 record vote in official minutes) deprioritizing abortion investigations. Endorsed 2025 by Stonewall Democrats of Dallas, Dallas Gay & Lesbian Alliance PAC, and Turn Texas Green (Ballotpedia); marched in the 2025 Dallas Pride Parade 'to show his allyship'; on Abbott's rainbow-crosswalk removal directive (Oct 2025): 'sexual orientation is not an ideology, gender identity is not an ideology.' Defund-era record: voted to divert $10M from DPD overtime to reserve (2021) and was one of 3 NO votes on restoring it; called 2024 Prop U 'draconian.' Opposed the DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership: 'I will not support diverting local resources toward a political or partisan agenda' (killed Nov 6, 2025). Authored the 2023 short-term-rental zoning ban (intra-city, not scored). Resisted the state's crosswalk directive — not credited under refuse_state_overreach since the rubric rewards resisting unconstitutional/ungodly mandates. No findable positions on schools, religious liberty, elections, or guns — left null. Religion not stated.

0.note: Seconded the motion and voted YES on the Aug 10, 2022 GRACE Act (official minutes record vote, 13-1) deprioritizing abortion-related investigations — documented contrary to affirming life from conception.

1.note: Voted to deprioritize abortion enforcement — documented contrary to abolition.

0.note: Accepted 2025 endorsements from Stonewall Democrats of Dallas and Dallas Gay & Lesbian Alliance PAC (also Stonewall-endorsed 2021, 2023) — single-issue endorsements contrary to a man-woman-only marriage affirmation.

2.note: 'Sexual orientation is not an ideology, gender identity is not an ideology' (Oct 2025, defending rainbow crosswalks against the state directive) — documented contrary to rejecting transgender ideology.

4.note: Marched in the 2025 Dallas Pride Parade 'to show his allyship'; publicly defended Pride crosswalks as 'a symbol of pride and inclusion' — documented contrary to opposing LGBTQ+ promotion in public policy.

0.note: Voted to divert $10M from DPD overtime (Sept 2021) and was one of 3 NO votes against restoring it ('It doesn't help to just throw money at an overtime fund'); called the 2024 Prop U police-staffing measure 'draconian.' No affirmative pro-police advocacy found.

1.note: His 287(g) opposition was framed on local-resources grounds; no direct statement on deportation policy — left null.

2.note: Opposed the $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership: 'I will not support diverting local resources toward a political or partisan agenda' (killed by unanimous committee vote Nov 6, 2025) — documented contrary to local-federal immigration-enforcement cooperation.

profile.education: B.A. International Economics, Texas Tech University
profile.background: Grew up in Snyder, TX; ~25 years in Dallas politics — deputy chief of staff to Mayor Tom Leppert, chief of staff to Mayor Mike Rawlings, senior advisor to the Dallas ISD superintendent. First elected 2019; re-elected May 3, 2025 (77.9% over Ernest Banda) — fourth and final term under Dallas's 4-term limit. Chaired the Environment & Sustainability Committee (CECAP climate plan). D9 = east Dallas/White Rock Lake/Lakewood.

paula-blackmon.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: re-elected May 3, 2025 with 77.9%; term-limited after this term. Voted YES on the Aug 10, 2022 GRACE Act and spoke for it against McGough — deciding whether to terminate the pregnancy 'is the purpose of the resolution.' One of seven co-sponsors of the Sept 2020 amendment cutting $7M from DPD overtime (defund-era, redirected to civilian hires/lighting); opposed 2024 Prop U (blamed HERO measures for Chief Garcia's exit; warned of >$175M cost); mixed later record — backed interim Chief Igo's 300-officer hiring plan (Feb 2025), but no affirmative pro-police advocacy found. Joined the Oct 2025 joint statement against DPD joining ICE 287(g) ('would turn local law enforcement into an arm of federal immigration enforcement... a betrayal of trust'); committee killed it Nov 6, 2025. Stonewall Democrats of Dallas endorsement (Ballotpedia). Championed CECAP (Paris-aligned climate plan, 2020) — no matching rubric cell, noted only. No findable positions on trans policy, schools, religious liberty, elections, state-overreach cells, or guns — left null. Religion not stated.

0.note: Voted YES on the Aug 10, 2022 GRACE Act and defended it against McGough: getting to decide whether to terminate the pregnancy 'is the purpose of the resolution' — documented contrary to affirming life from conception.

1.note: Advocated deprioritizing abortion enforcement — documented contrary to abolition.

0.note: Endorsed by Stonewall Democrats of Dallas (LGBTQ Democratic org) — single-issue endorsement contrary to a man-woman-only marriage affirmation.

2.note: No trans-specific statements found — left null.

4.note: Accepted Stonewall Democrats endorsement; attended Pride flag ceremony per Dallas Observer coverage — contrary to opposing LGBTQ+ promotion in public policy.

0.note: Co-sponsored the Sept 2020 amendment cutting $7M from DPD overtime (defund-era); opposed the 2024 Prop U police-staffing measure. Later backed the chief's 300-officer hiring plan (Feb 2025) — mixed, but the documented record is the defund-era cut and no affirmative pro-police advocacy.

1.note: Joined the joint statement that 287(g) 'would turn local law enforcement into an arm of federal immigration enforcement and could result in a betrayal of trust' — documented contrary to supporting mandatory deportation.

2.note: Publicly opposed and helped block the $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership (Oct-Nov 2025) — documented contrary to local-federal immigration-enforcement cooperation.

profile.religion: Baptist (former Executive Pastor of Ministries, Concord Church, 2021-2023)
profile.education: B.S. Accounting, University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff; M.A. Organizational Leadership, Luther Rice Seminary; Harvard JFK School state/local govt certificate
profile.background: ~16 years in Dallas City Hall finance roles (Debt Manager, Cash & Investment Manager, Director of the Office of Business Diversity 2017-2021); municipal finance at First Southwest Co.; Executive Pastor of Ministries at Concord Church 2021-2023. First elected June 2023 (runoff over Joe Tave); re-elected May 3, 2025 (~54%, no runoff). Married to Erin; two adopted sons. D3 = southwest Dallas/west Oak Cliff.

zarin-gracey.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local tier). Verified current: re-elected May 3, 2025 (~54% over Lightbourne and Sims, no runoff); term ends 2027. Openly Christian public official — X bio: 'Through service to my church and my City, I desire to boldly reflect Christ through out the City of Dallas'; former Concord Church executive pastor; a Dallas LGBT-activist publication flagged him as religiously conservative, and Stonewall Democrats' 2023 endorsement list skipped District 3 (suggestive, not scored). Fiscally frustrated with the Prop U mandate while voting for the 300-officer hiring goal (Feb 2025: 'This silly, silly loop is so extremely frustrating') — said he favors more officers (paraphrase, not credited under the high-bar rule). Voted yes on the unanimous FY2024-25 budget and (unnamed in dissent coverage) the FY2025-26 budget. Attended the Nov 6, 2025 joint-committee session that unanimously killed the $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership (only West absent — joined the unanimous rejection, no individual statement). No findable positions on abortion, marriage/trans, schools, elections, state-overreach cells, or guns — left null.

0.note: Public X bio as a sitting councilman: 'Through service to my church and my City, I desire to boldly reflect Christ through out the City of Dallas' — affirmative public profession of Christ in public office.

Said he favored more officers (paraphrase) while criticizing the Prop U mandate as fiscally irrational; voted for hiring goals and budgets — routine governance plus a soft paraphrase, below the affirmative-advocacy bar. Null.

1.note: No individual statement on deportation policy found — left null.

2.note: Nov 6, 2025 joint-committee vote killing the $25M DPD-ICE 287(g) partnership was unanimous among the 13 of 14 councilmembers attending; only West was absent — Gracey attended and joined the unanimous rejection of local-federal immigration-enforcement cooperation.
